channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: King and Queen Spend a Day in New York
item.description: During their state visit, King Charles III and Queen Camilla stopped by New York City, where they laid flowers at the 9/11 memorial, fed chickens, read to children and attended a charity event.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Michael Bloomberg, left, King Charles III and Queen Camilla laid flowers at the 9/11 memorial on Wednesday morning, the first of several stops during the royal couple’s visit to New York City.

item.title: ¿Por qué los restaurantes de sushi más exclusivos nos dejan aburridos y sin dinero?
item.description: Lo que empezó en Japón como una comida rápida y emocionante para la clase trabajadora, se ha transformado en un elaborado capricho para comensales adinerados en otros lugares.
item.dc:creator: Ligaya Mishan

media:content.media:credit: Ellen Silverman para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: En muchos restaurantes omakase estadounidenses, la cuenta será muy elevada y los bocados serán, en su mayoría, opciones complacientes y poco arriesgadas para el público.

item.title: Picture Prompt: Ticking Watch
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Trump pone a prueba los límites de sus partidarios más devotos
item.description: La retórica religiosa del presidente, las políticas migratorias de línea dura y la guerra de EE. UU. en Irán ha dividido a una coalición de votantes cristianos que lo devolvieron a la Casa Blanca.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Líderes de la comunidad latina imponiendo sus manos a Trump en Miami antes de las elecciones de 2024.

item.title: Today’s Spelling Bee Hints for April 30, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Josh Johnson Gives King Charles Props for His Comedy Chops
item.description: “You know, I’ve always wondered what white Def Jam would be like,” Johnson said after King Charles cracked a few jokes at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día: ‘intermittent’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 219 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Cardio o fuerza. ¿Debo hacer los dos?
item.description: Expertos en ejercicio y medicina deportiva nos explican qué sucede si te enfocas exclusivamente en fortalecer tus músculos.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas para The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: intermittent
item.description: This word has appeared in 119 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: 5 conclusiones del testimonio de Hegseth sobre la guerra en Irán y su gestión
item.description: Fue la primera comparecencia pública del secretario de Defensa Pete Hegseth ante el Congreso desde que comenzó la operación Furia Épica a finales de febrero.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Megan Mineiro
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:description: El secretario de Defensa, Pete Hegseth, y el general Dan Caine declararon durante una audiencia ante el Congreso, el miércoles.

item.title: J. Craig Venter, Scientist Who Decoded the Human Genome, Dies at 79
item.description: A risk-taking outsider, he brought speed, competition and controversy to one of science’s biggest races.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Wade

media:content.media:credit: Sandy Huffaker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: J. Craig Venter, next to Algae growth in his greenhouse in La Jolla, Calif., competed with a rival team of scientists in assembling the first human genome.

item.title: Oil Prices Soar as Iran Standoff Shows No End in Sight
item.description: Oil prices push higher, approaching another wartime high, as President Trump asserted that the naval blockade of Iran’s ports would persist.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Rising Fuel Prices Could Force Excruciating Choices on Economic Policies
item.description: The European Central Bank and Bank of England were expected to hold interest rates steady on Thursday while searching for signs of possible longer-term damage.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Violette Franchi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A gas station in Paris. The war in Iran had an almost immediate impact on European inflation, increasing gasoline prices as well as airfares and other fuel-intensive activities.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Blood Feuds Have Faded As Rivals Play Nice(r)
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, April 30, 2026.

item.title: Mother Runs for Office in India After Daughter Was Raped and Killed
item.description: Ratna Debnath is a candidate for Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party, which is campaigning on women’s safety in an Indian state election.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das and Hari Kumar

media:content.media:credit: Atul Loke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ratna Debnath, center, campaigning in the North 24 Parganas district in West Bengal this month.

item.title: In France, Trump’s Triumphal Arch Draws Eye Rolls
item.description: The Arc de Triomphe in Paris, which the president vows to top in Washington, offers a lesson in the complicated history of monuments.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Conceived in 1806 by Napoleon, the Arc de Triomphe has come to symbolize very different things in modern France.

item.title: Corrections: April 30, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, April 30, 2026.

item.title: House Adopts Budget to Unlock $70 Billion for Immigration Enforcement
item.description: The measure will allow the G.O.P. to begin working on a filibuster-proof bill to fund ICE and C.B.P., part of their plan to reopen the long-shuttered Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Federal agents outside a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement facility in Chicago last year.

item.title: D4vd Bought Chain Saws to Dispose of Celeste Rivas Hernandez’s Body, Prosecutors Say
item.description: In a legal filing, prosecutors outlined for the first time how they say the singer murdered and dismembered Celeste Rivas Hernandez.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens, Emmanuel Morgan and Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A neighbor of Celeste Rivas Hernandez’s at a memorial in Lake Elsinore, Calf.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 30, 2026
item.description: Lance Enfinger and John Kugelman land themselves a prizewinning puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: Catch dinner and a movie after solving this Thursday puzzle.

item.title: Supreme Court Considers Trump Administration’s Plan to End TPS
item.description: The justices on Wednesday considered whether the Trump administration had legally ended the Temporary Protected Status program for hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians.
item.dc:creator: Brent McDonald, Whitney Shefte, Ang Li and Yasu Tsuji

item.title: The War in Iran Has Cost $25 Billion So Far. Is That a Lot?
item.description: It exceeds the annual budgets of numerous federal agencies, and the Pentagon’s yearly budget for munitions.
item.dc:creator: Margot Sanger-Katz, Alicia Parlapiano and Emily Badger

item.title: British Royals Crisscross Manhattan in Brief Visit Packed With Photo Ops
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla laid flowers at the Sept. 11 memorial before stopping by an urban farm, the New York Public Library, a business event and a gala.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear, Claire Fahy and Sarah Lyall
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Michael Bloomberg, left, King Charles III and Queen Camilla laid flowers at the 9/11 memorial on Wednesday morning, the first of several stops during the royal couple’s visit to New York City.

item.title: Mamdani’s Advice to Royals: Give That Priceless Diamond Back to India
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and King Charles did not meet privately. But if they had, the mayor said, he probably would have raised the issue of the Koh-i-Noor Diamond.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York speaking with King Charles III at the 9/11 memorial on Wednesday.

item.title: What Trump’s 250-Foot Triumphal Arch in D.C. Would Look Like
item.description: The Times created a 3-D model of the 250-foot monument to show how it would affect a symbolic sightline.
item.dc:creator: Junho Lee, Doug Mills, Karthik Patanjali, Anushka Patil and Charlie Smart

item.title: Sheriff of New Orleans Jail Where 10 Inmates Escaped Is Indicted
item.description: Susan Hutson, the sheriff of Orleans Parish, faces 30 counts, and the jail’s chief financial officer was also indicted.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Brett Duke/The Advocate, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Sheriff Susan Hutson after a news conference in 2025 following the jailbreak. She has been indicted on 30 counts of misconduct related to the escape.

item.title: Trump Administration Announces Moves to Sharply Curtail Gun Regulations
item.description: The changes are in keeping with the administration’s pledge to cut back on gun restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.category: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Visitors looked over weapons displayed at the Crossroads of the West Gun Show in Phoenix in January.

item.title: Trump Threatens to Pull Troops From Germany as He Lashes Out at Chancellor
item.description: The comments came after the German chancellor said that Iran has “humiliated” the United States.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: U.S. soldiers on a combat exercise in Bavaria, Germany.

item.title: Powell Announces He Will Stay on as Fed Governor
item.description: Jerome H. Powell announced on Wednesday that he would stay on as a governor at the Federal Reserve after his term as chair ends next month due to lingering legal threats from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jerome H. Powell on Wednesday said that he would remain at the central bank after his term as chair ends on May 15 and his successor, Kevin M. Warsh, takes over.

item.title: Roger Sweet, Creator of the He-Man Action Figure, Dies at 91
item.description: The musclebound character he developed as a toy designer for Mattel gave rise to the Masters of the Universe franchise and helped define the machismo of the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

item.title: What the Royal State Dinner Guest List Says About Trump’s America
item.description: There were at least 10 American billionaires, six Fox News hosts, assorted presidential pals, no Democratic politicians and not so many British.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Bumiller
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain

media:content.media:description: President Trump and Melania Trump with King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: They Left for the School Bus. ICE Picked Them Up Instead.
item.description: A school transfer disrupted two brothers’ visas, their lawyer said, leaving them vulnerable to arrest and unsettling their Mississippi school community.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: The Justices Acted as Partisans in the Voting Rights Ruling
item.description: In the name of disentangling race from politics, the court has given white voters more power at the expense of racial minorities.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board

item.title: BossMan Dlow's Boisterous Boasts
item.description: On this edition of the “Popcast” Song of the Week, our critic Jon Caramanica breaks down a boastful party track from BossMan Dlow, who he calls “the funniest rapper working right now.”
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: Why the U.A.E. is Quitting OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is walking away from OPEC this May. The New York Times’ energy reporter, Rebecca Elliott, breaks down how the war with Iran provided the perfect opening for the U.A.E. to go solo.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Nour Idriss, Jon Miller, Stephanie Swart and Paul Abowd
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries

item.title: 40 Years After Explosion, Chernobyl Site Faces New Threats From Russia
item.description: Forty years since the Chernobyl nuclear disaster, officials are grappling with the impact of a 2025 Russian drone strike that set back decades of efforts to contain it. Ukrainian officials said the Russians deliberately targeted the structure, but the Kremlin has denied responsibility.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

item.title: G.O.P. Congress Struggles to Do the Basics Amid Party Infighting
item.description: House Republicans toiled to pass major measures as lawmakers tried to fund homeland security, extend spy powers and deliver a farm bill.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse

media:content.media:description: “Right now we don’t look as functional as we need to look,” Representative Tom Cole, Republican of Oklahoma, acknowledged.

item.title: Mississippi Middle School Students Stop Bus After Driver Blacks Out
item.description: Surveillance video shows a group of middle school students in southern Mississippi taking control of a school bus on a highway after their driver blacked out from an asthma attack.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Hancock County School District, via Associated Press

item.title: Hegseth Cites Falsehood to Defend His Firing of Senior Officers
item.description: The defense secretary said at a House hearing that President Barack Obama had fired 197 generals, a figure that the Pentagon previously acknowledged was false.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s actions to fire senior military leaders are without precedent in recent decades and have come with little explanation.

item.title: These Runners Kept Going to 100 or Beyond
item.description: Many picked up running late in life. They all shared an uncommon competitive drive.
item.dc:creator: Amisha Padnani

media:content.media:credit: Brit Huckabay/National Senior Games Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Julia Hawkins competing in the 50-meter dash at the National Senior Games in Albuquerque in 2019.

item.title: Lester Wright, the Fastest Known Centenarian, Dies at 103
item.description: In 2022, at 100, he set what was believed to be a world record in the 100-meter dash at the Penn Relays in Philadelphia, beating 86- and 92-year-old competitors.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

item.title: Mississippi Middle School Students Avert Bus Crash After Driver Loses Control
item.description: Footage of the incident shared this week by a school district in Mississippi shows a group of students working together to avert disaster on a highway.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel

media:content.media:credit: Hancock County School District, via Associated Press

item.title: Queen Camilla Unites Winnie-the-Pooh With a Long-Lost Friend
item.description: On Wednesday, the Queen of England presented the New York Public Library with a bespoke replica of Roo, the smallest companion of the Bear of Very Little Brain.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyall
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Queen Camilla presents a specially made replica of Roo, the baby kangaroo companion of Winnie-the-Pooh, to Anthony Marx, the president and chief executive of the New York Public Library.

item.title: Brown University Gunman Planned Attack for Years, F.B.I. Says
item.description: Investigators said the man’s transient lifestyle and social isolation made his intentions hard to track before shootings that killed two Brown students and an M.I.T. professor.
item.dc:creator: Mark Arsenault

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial on the Brown University campus, where a gunman killed two people in December.

item.title: Pentagon Estimates Iran War Cost at $25 Billion
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rebuffed criticism from lawmakers over the Iran war during a budget hearing on Wednesday. The defense department requested nearly $1.45 trillion for the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Pete Hegseth, the defense secretary, during a hearing at the Capitol on Wednesday.

item.title: Jury Delivers Mixed Verdict in Case of Afghan Charged in 2021 Kabul Attack
item.description: Jurors found the man guilty of conspiracy to support the terrorist group that took credit for a horrific bombing, but deadlocked on whether he was responsible for the attack itself.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Jim Huylebroek for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People being sent away from the scene of a bomb blast outside the airport in Kabul, Afghanistan, on Aug. 26, 2021.

item.title: Surveillance Law Is in Limbo After House Approval
item.description: Republicans put down a right-wing revolt to push a three-year renewal through the House, but the Senate appeared likely to opt for a 45-day punt ahead of a Friday expiration.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage and Michael Gold
item.category: Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods

media:content.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson struggled for more than two hours on Wednesday to round up the votes to bring up the surveillance bill.

item.title: Supreme Court Limits Reach of Voting Rights Act
item.description: Also, the Pentagon estimates the Iran war cost at $25 billion. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Nine Artists on the Gardens They’ll Never Forget
item.description: Hiroshi Sugimoto, Irene Neuwirth, Umberto Pasti and others share their impressions of mesmerizing plots all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Michael Snyder

item.title: Supreme Court Updates: Justices Further Weaken Voting Rights Act, Igniting Political Scramble
item.description: The court’s conservative majority said it had upheld the landmark law, as liberal justices accused the court of gutting the act. In striking down a Louisiana voting map as a racial gerrymander, the court opened the door for other states to redraw maps.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
